step_by_step_thinking:
Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC) typically presents with distinct histological features that differentiate it from other skin cancers. One hallmark finding on biopsy is nuclear palisading, which refers to the arrangement of tumor cells around small blood vessels or collagen bundles in a manner resembling a picket fence. This pattern helps clinicians identify BCC as part of its diagnostic criteria.

The correct answer is C: Nuclear palisading. Clinical Reasoning Complete.
